title: Registering a duplicate route panics instead of returning an error
body: Calling router.Handle twice for the same method+path hits `panic("a handle is already registered for path '" + fullPath + "'")` in addRoute. For an app that registers routes dynamically from a config file, we'd rather get an error back from Handle() than have the whole process crash on a duplicate.

title: Params slice isn't returned to the pool when a handler panics
body: ServeHTTP calls r.recv() via defer to recover from handler panics, but the code path that normally calls r.putParams(ps) after a successful handle() is skipped when recover() kicks in, so under frequent panics the paramsPool keeps allocating new slices instead of reusing them.

title: CleanPath behavior with mixed '..' and '//' needs examples
body: The package docs mention that RedirectFixedPath removes 'superfluous path elements like ../ or //' but I couldn't find a worked example of something like `/a/../..//b` to know whether it resolves to `/b` or 404s. A couple of CleanPath examples in path.go's doc comment would help.

title: Wildcard conflict panic messages don't show which existing route conflicts
body: When insertChild hits a wildcard conflict it panics with the new path but for a router with hundreds of registered routes it's hard to tell at a glance which previously-registered route is the culprit. Could the panic message include the full existing route, not just the offending prefix?
